content:
ON FORMAT & WORKFLOW (We Arrive at It Together)

Here’s what I know we need:

- Weekly or bi-weekly sync calls (30–90 minutes depending on phase) to process what we’re learning. We don’t script these; we let them breathe.
- Shared documentation (Google Docs, Figma, GitHub—whatever feels natural to you). I don’t care about system; I care about your thinking being preserved.
- Access to prototyping infrastructure: You can test ideas in real time; see how parameter changes affect narrative feel. You need to experience the system, not just theorize about it.
- Audience feedback loops: We test with small groups (10–20 people) regularly so you can observe how readers/viewers actually respond to different parameter configurations. The audience is a collaborator too.
- Flexibility on timeline: If you need to step back for two weeks to let something simmer, that’s fine. This is exploratory work; it doesn’t follow a Gantt chart.

What I don’t know—and genuinely want to figure out with you:

- Do you prefer synchronous work (calls, collaborative writing sessions) or asynchronous (you work independently, we synthesize weekly)?
